core_behavior: Implementing robust error handling mechanisms to detect, report, and recover from errors
Using reliable algorithms and data structures to prevent data corruption or loss
Ensuring that the system can withstand expected and unexpected inputs, loads, and environmental conditions
Providing mechanisms for monitoring, logging, and analyzing system performance and errors to facilitate debugging and maintenance
inputs: User input (e.g., commands, data, configuration settings)
Environmental inputs (e.g., network connectivity, hardware resources)
Scheduled or periodic inputs (e.g., cron jobs, timer events)
outputs: User-facing output (e.g., display screens, reports, alerts)
assumptions: The system will be designed and implemented using a modular, component-based architecture to facilitate fault isolation and recovery
The system will use redundant components or services where possible to ensure continued operation in the event of a failure
The system will be regularly updated and maintained to ensure that known vulnerabilities and issues are addressed
The system will be designed to fail safely, preventing damage to data, hardware, or other systems in the event of a failure
failure_modes: Detect and report the failure to the user or system administrator
Attempt to recover from the failure using automated mechanisms (e.g., retrying failed operations, switching to redundant components)
Provide diagnostic information to facilitate debugging and maintenance
Prevent data loss or corruption by using transactional mechanisms or backups
Fail safely, preventing damage to hardware or other systems
edge_cases: Power outages or other environmental disruptions
Simultaneous failures of multiple components or services
security_constraints: Implement secure coding practices and secure protocols for communication
Use secure storage and encryption mechanisms for sensitive data
Authenticate and authorize users and services to prevent unauthorized access
Regularly update and patch dependencies and components to prevent exploitation of known vulnerabilities
performance_constraints: Respond to user input and requests within a reasonable time frame (e.g., 1-2 seconds)
Maintain a high level of throughput and processing capacity under expected loads
Use caching, buffering, or other optimization techniques to minimize latency and improve performance
Monitor and report performance metrics to facilitate tuning and optimization
